howItWorks.intro: This print bed area calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Usable Area = (Bed Width - 2 × Margin) × (Bed Depth - 2 × Margin)
Usable Volume = Usable Width × Usable Depth × Height ÷ 1000
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a good print bed size for beginners?
faq.answer: 220×220mm is the most popular size for beginners (Ender 3 class). It's large enough for most projects while being affordable. 300×300mm gives more flexibility for larger prints.

faq.question: Why do I need an edge margin?
faq.answer: The outer 3-10mm of most print beds have uneven heating or clips that prevent printing. Keeping a 5mm margin ensures reliable first-layer adhesion.

faq.question: Can I print objects larger than my build volume?
faq.answer: Yes, by splitting the model into pieces and assembling after printing. Design interlocking joints or use adhesive to join sections.
